video_title: Electric Toothbrushes Explained : Sonic vs Rotating Oscillating Electric Toothbrush
comment_text: I've been using Sonic toothbrushes for years and I got to tell you. They only last 1 to 2 years. For some reason the motor dislodges from the stick that hangs on to the brush. That stick ends up barely holding on to the motor and weakening. It happened to every single Sonic toothbrush I've ever owned. No matter how well I take care of it it will break down guaranteed almost as if it was preemptively created to break in 1 and 1/2 years. I swear, I'm not kidding. I want to try Oral-B to see if it's the same in that case. I'm tired of paying another $100 for these motors every couple years. I already pay tons in the attachment brushes.

video_title: Best Electric Toothbrushes in 2024 (Dental Hygienist Explains)
comment_text: I love love my Equate rechargeable toothbrush . $18 and verry powerful and basic . Gotten great results with my dental hygenist . The Oral B basic one at $18 is a bit underpowered for me . Yeah they recently stopped carrying it at Walmart but found it at eBay . I already have one but since it's discontinued decided to get a new one . The first one has lasted a verry long time like five years .. Equate infinity Pro for the win
